content.ctaHeading: Stop losing signed-in carts across devices.
content.ctaBody: Add Persistent Cart in one click — free to start, nothing for your shoppers to do.
trust.impact: {amount} in orders from synced carts — last 90 days
hero.eyebrow: The original cross-device cart app for Shopify
hero.h1.turn: On a default Shopify store, their cart doesn't follow.
hero.sub: A shopper fills a cart on their phone, opens your store on their laptop — and the cart is empty. No error, no email, just a lost sale. Persistent Cart fixes it in one click (plus one switch in your theme editor): every signed-in cart follows the customer to every device, the way Amazon and Walmart already work. Nothing for shoppers to do.
hero.demo.caption: Same shopper, same login — phone to laptop. On default Shopify the cart stays behind. With Persistent Cart it's already there.
demo.cart.title: Your cart
trustbar.lead: Trusted by thousands of Shopify stores — including hundreds on Shopify Plus.
footer.tagline: Keep a signed-in shopper's cart on every device — the cross-device cart sync the biggest stores already use, brought to Shopify.

page.home.title: Persistent Cart — cross-device cart sync for Shopify (Built for Shopify)
page.home.desc: Shoppers switch devices; on a default Shopify store the cart doesn't follow. Persistent Cart keeps a signed-in cart on every device — one click, no popups or emails. 4.9★, Built for Shopify, since 2016.
home.trust.lead: The original cross-device cart app for Shopify — keeping signed-in carts in sync since 2016.
home.problem.eyebrow: The leak most owners never see
home.problem.heading: Your store probably drops a signed-in cart the moment a shopper switches devices.
home.problem.intro: On a default Shopify store, the cart lives in a browser cookie — not on the customer's account. So when a logged-in shopper adds items on their phone at lunch and opens your store on a laptop that night, the cart is empty. Most owners never see it happen, because nobody emails to say "my cart vanished." They just don't check out.
home.problem.without.label: Default Shopify
home.problem.without.title: The cart stays on one device
home.problem.without.body: Phone cart and laptop cart are two separate carts. Switch device, lose the cart. No error, no email — just a smaller order or no order.
home.problem.with.label: With Persistent Cart
home.problem.with.title: The cart follows the customer
home.problem.with.body: The signed-in cart syncs across phone, laptop, and return visits automatically — the same way the biggest retailers already work.
home.problem.aha: It's the difference between a cart that belongs to a browser and a cart that belongs to your customer.
home.problem.stat: 9 in 10 people move between devices to get things done, and 88% of shoppers expect a consistent experience across devices and channels.
home.problem.statSource: Google/Ipsos, 2012 · Bazaarvoice Shopper Experience Index, Nov 2024
home.benefits.heading: Three things start working the way shoppers already expect.
home.benefits.1.title: Recover carts you never knew you were losing
home.benefits.1.body: Every signed-in cart that used to vanish on a device switch now survives to checkout — carts your email flows never even saw, because the shopper never abandoned, they just changed screens.
home.benefits.2.title: Bigger orders, because carts keep growing
home.benefits.2.body: An uninterrupted cross-device cart accumulates. Shoppers add on mobile, add more on desktop, and check out once — instead of rebuilding a smaller cart from memory.
home.benefits.3.title: One click, and nothing changes for shoppers
home.benefits.3.body: No popups, no emails, no opt-ins, nothing for customers to do. It runs silently in the background — so quietly that merchants tell us they forget it's on.
home.motion.heading: The same signed-in cart, on every device.
home.motion.intro: A logged-in shopper adds an item on their phone. Later they open your store on a laptop, signed in — and the cart is already there, matching, with no prompt to deal with. This is the default behavior: a silent auto-merge.
home.motion.note: By default Persistent Cart merges carts silently. Prefer to let shoppers choose? An optional "keep or combine" prompt is one toggle away.
home.parity.eyebrow: How the biggest stores already do this
home.parity.heading: Amazon and Walmart keep a signed-in cart on every device. Shoppers expect yours to, too.
home.parity.intro: This isn't a nice-to-have feature — it's the baseline experience at the stores your customers shop most. Their own help pages say so:
home.parity.walmart.name: Walmart
home.parity.walmart.quote: A Walmart account lets you "access your cart across multiple devices."
home.parity.amazon.name: Amazon
home.parity.amazon.quote: "Items added to your Shopping Cart will be available from any compatible web browser or Amazon Mobile app that has been signed in to your account."
home.parity.shopify.name: Default Shopify
home.parity.shopify.quote: The cart is stored in a browser cookie, so a signed-in customer's cart doesn't follow them to another device or browser.
home.parity.shopify.source: Our summary — based on Shopify's cookie policy + community
home.parity.cta: Learn what a persistent cart is — the full explainer
home.parity.diagram.caption: A signed-in cart lives on the customer's account — reachable from any device — instead of on one browser.
home.metrics.heading: Not a 2025 experiment. A 2016 utility that stores keep paying for.
home.metrics.1.label: Order value from synced carts
home.metrics.1.note: in the last 90 days, across stores using the app

home.testimonial.quote: Without the app, customers lose their shopping cart if they switch devices… This is NOT how all of the large online retailer websites function… the cart effectively stays with the Customer ACCOUNT rather than just the DEVICE.
home.testimonial.author: Green Mountain Diapers
home.testimonial.meta: United States · using Persistent Cart for 9+ years
home.testimonial.cta: Read all {count} reviews on the App Store
home.compare.heading: It's the missing half of your abandonment strategy.
home.compare.intro: Abandoned-cart emails and SMS fire after a cart is gone, and only reach shoppers who opted in. Persistent Cart works before abandonment — it keeps the cart from disappearing in the first place, for every signed-in shopper. They work best together.
home.compare.row.when.label: When it acts
home.compare.row.when.persistent: Before abandonment — the cart never disappears
home.compare.row.when.recovery: After abandonment — tries to win the shopper back
home.compare.row.who.label: Who it reaches
home.compare.row.action.label: Shopper has to
home.compare.row.action.recovery: Open a message and click back
home.compare.row.cost.label: Per-message cost
home.compare.footnote: Persistent Cart complements your email tool — it recovers the carts your flows never see.
home.calc.heading: How much could cross-device cart loss be costing you?
home.calc.intro: Adjust a few inputs — pre-filled with illustrative defaults — for a transparent, illustrative range. We show the formula; nothing is a black box.
home.audit.eyebrow: Not sure if it affects you?
home.audit.heading: We'll check your store for cross-device cart loss — free.
home.audit.body: Send us your store and we'll tell you whether a signed-in cart survives a device switch, roughly what it could be costing, and the quick wins — no obligation, no install required.
home.b2b.heading: Long, high-value carts that have to survive a session switch.
home.b2b.body: Wholesale and B2B buyers build big carts across sessions and devices — and a dropped cart at that size is a real order lost. Persistent Cart keeps large signed-in carts intact across devices, and handles shared workstations without leaking one buyer's cart to another.
home.partner.heading: Earn 30% recurring for 12 months on every store you refer.
home.partner.body: It's an easy recommendation: one click, no customer disruption, a hidden leak fixed — and recurring commission for you. Built for Shopify, trusted since 2016.
home.data.heading: It accesses only what it needs to sync a cart.
home.data.body: Persistent Cart works for signed-in customers. To sync a cart it stores the cart's contents against the customer's account for your store — nothing more than what a cart contains. Guests aren't tracked. We're Built for Shopify and follow Shopify's required data-protection flows.
home.data.point.1: Works only for logged-in customers — no guest tracking
home.data.point.2: Stores cart contents to sync them, scoped to your store
home.data.point.3: No emails sent, no contacts captured, no consent prompts
home.pricing.heading: Every feature, on every plan. Priced to your Shopify plan.
home.pricing.intro: There are no feature tiers to decode — every plan includes the full app. Your price simply matches your Shopify plan. Start free, then a 30-day trial on any paid plan.
home.pricing.trialNote: Free Starter plan · 30-day free trial on paid plans
home.faq.heading: What merchants ask before installing.
home.faq.q1.q: Doesn't Shopify already sync carts across devices?
home.faq.q1.a: No. On a default Shopify store the cart is stored in a browser cookie, not on the customer's account — so a signed-in shopper's cart doesn't follow them to another device or browser. Shopify's own community confirms this needs custom code or an app. Persistent Cart is that fix.
home.faq.q2.q: Will my customers see popups or get emails?
home.faq.q2.a: No. By default Persistent Cart merges carts silently in the background — no popup, no email, no opt-in, nothing for the shopper to do. An optional "keep or combine" prompt is available if you'd rather let shoppers choose, but it's off by default.
home.faq.q3.q: Does it work for guests, or only logged-in customers?
home.faq.q3.a: It works for logged-in (identified) customers — that's what makes cross-device sync possible, because the cart can be tied to the account. Guests browsing without an account aren't tracked.
home.faq.q4.q: How hard is it to install?
home.faq.q4.a: One click to install from the Shopify App Store, then enable the app embed in your theme editor — no theme code, no developer. It starts working immediately.
home.faq.q5.q: Will it slow down my store?
home.faq.q5.a: It runs lightweight in the background and is Built for Shopify, which means it meets Shopify's performance and quality standards. Most merchants never notice it's running — which is the point.
home.faq.q6.q: How is this different from my abandoned-cart email app?
home.faq.q6.a: Those tools act after a cart is abandoned and only reach opted-in contacts. Persistent Cart acts before — it keeps the cart from disappearing when a signed-in shopper switches devices. They're complementary: use both.
home.faq.q7.q: How do I know it's worth it for my store?
home.faq.q7.a: Test it on your own store before paying: the Free Starter plan runs up to 10 real cart syncs, and any paid plan adds a 30-day trial. On Basic it's $4.99/month — recovering a single mid-sized cross-device order more than covers it. You can watch it work on your own store before a dollar is charged, so you're not guessing.
home.faq.q8.q: What does it do with my customers' data?
home.faq.q8.a: The minimum a cart sync needs. It works only for logged-in customers and stores the cart's contents against the customer's account for your store — nothing more than what a cart contains. Guests aren't tracked, no emails are sent, no consent prompts appear, and the data stays scoped to your store. We're Built for Shopify and follow Shopify's required data-protection flows.
home.final.heading: Give your customers the cart they expect — on every device.
home.final.body: Add Persistent Cart in one click — from that moment, signed-in carts follow your customers everywhere.
home.final.trust: 4.9★ · Built for Shopify · Trusted since 2016 · 30-day free trial on paid plans
